title: microclimalt
text:
Introduction
microclimalt is a project that aims to exploit the micrometeorological loggers installed by the ALT project in 2023 to improve our understanding of the horizontal, vertical and temporal variations in the microclimate of tropical forests.
The objective are:

title: microclimalt
text:
Project
microclimalt includes all codes to access the data (in data/) & analyse the data (files.qmd) with associated documentation and figures. Intermediary files and outputs can be accessed in outputs/. The project takes advantage of renv to manage the R environment for enhanced reproducibility.

To be reviewed later splitting raw and derived data.

title: Data
text:
Acquisitions were made at two sites: Paracou, where LiDAR data was available for comparison, and Nouragues, where the hydrology was potentially different. For the moment, however, we will focus on Paracou. The sensors were located across transects that covered topographical gradients (22) and forest canopy openness (10). The sensors were spaced 10 m apart, with a minimum distance of 1 m from the HOBO sensors to avoid ground disturbance caused by the HOBO stakes. Acquisition began on 1 May 2023. The sensors were Tomst TMS-4s (https://tomst.com/web/en/systems/tms/tms-4/) with a temporal resolution of 15 minutes and protection against direct light and rain (although they still overheated in the sunshine).


Code
infos <- read_csv("data/raw_data/micro/Tomst-HOBO sensors - Paracou - Data.csv",
         n_max = 32) %>% 
  select(-1) %>% 
  rename_all(tolower) %>% 
  mutate(neartreenum = paste0(treenum1, ":", dtree1, ";", 
                              treenum2, ":", dtree2, ";", 
                              treenum3, ":", dtree3, ";")) %>% 
  select(site, plot, subplot, tomstid, tomstsensornum, transect,
         lighthabitat, topohabitat, layonedge) %>% 
  rename(id = tomstid, sensornum = tomstsensornum, 
         light = lighthabitat, topography = topohabitat, layon = layonedge) %>% 
  mutate(light = recode(light, "Sunfleck" = "Understory"))
infos %>% 
  group_by(light, topography) %>% 
  summarise(N = n()) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(light, topography, label = N)) +
  geom_point(aes(size = N)) +
  ggrepel::geom_text_repel() +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.title = element_blank())





Tomst sampling design.






Code
alt <- read_csv("data/raw_data/inventory/ALT_Paracou9ha_20250129_cleancord.csv") %>% 
  select(IdTree, Xutm, Yutm) %>% 
  rename(id = IdTree, x = Xutm, y = Yutm) %>% 
  unique()
coords <- read_csv("data/raw_data/micro/Tomst-HOBO sensors - Paracou - Data.csv",
                   n_max = 32) %>% 
  select(TomstSensorNum, TreeNum1, TreeNum2, TreeNum3, DTree1, DTree2, DTree3) %>% 
  rename(sensornum = TomstSensorNum, 
         id_1 = TreeNum1, id_2 = TreeNum2, id_3 = TreeNum3,
         d_1 = DTree1, d_2 = DTree2, d_3 = DTree3) %>% 
  mutate(id_d__1 = paste(id_1, d_1),
         id_d__2 = paste(id_2, d_2),
         id_d__3 = paste(id_3, d_3)) %>% 
  select(sensornum, id_d__1, id_d__2, id_d__3) %>% 
  gather(variable, value, -sensornum) %>% 
  separate(value, c("id", "d"), convert = TRUE) %>% 
  separate(variable, c("id_d", "measure"), "__", convert = TRUE) %>% 
  arrange(sensornum, measure) %>% 
  select(-id_d) %>% 
  mutate(id = ifelse(str_length(id) < 6, 190000+id, id)) %>% 
  mutate(id = ifelse(
                  str_length(id) == 6,
                  paste0(str_sub(id, 1,2), "_", str_sub(id, 3,6)),
                  id
                )) %>% 
  left_join(alt) %>% 
  group_by(sensornum) %>% 
  do(center = paste(nlm(function(x){
      sqrt(sum((x[1]-.$x)^2+(x[2]-.$y)^2))-sum(.$d)
    }, c(mean(.$x), mean(.$y)))$estimate, collapse = ";")) %>% 
  unnest(center) %>% 
  separate(center, c("x", "y"), sep = ";") %>%
  ungroup()
write_tsv(coords, "data/derived_data/coords.tsv")
d <- read_tsv("data/derived_data/coords.tsv") %>% 
  left_join(infos) %>% 
  filter(id %in% 1:18) %>% 
  arrange(id) %>% 
  mutate(dx = x - lag(x), dy = y - lag(y)) %>% 
  na.omit() %>% 
  summarise(dx = mean(dx), dy = mean(dy))
coords <- read_tsv("data/derived_data/coords.tsv") %>% 
  left_join(infos) %>% 
  mutate(x = ifelse(id == 19, lag(x) + d$dx, x),
         y = ifelse(id == 19, lag(y) + d$dy, y)) %>% 
  mutate(x = ifelse(id == 21, lag(x) + d$dx, x),
         y = ifelse(id == 21, lag(y) + d$dy, y)) %>% 
  mutate(x = ifelse(id == 22, lag(x) + d$dx, x),
         y = ifelse(id == 22, lag(y) + d$dy, y))
write_tsv(coords, "data/derived_data/coords.tsv")




Code
read_tsv("data/derived_data/coords.tsv") %>% 
  st_as_sf(coords = c("x", "y"), crs = 32622) %>% 
  ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = read_sf("data/raw_data/inventory/ALT/ALT.shp"), fill = NA) +
  geom_sf(aes(col = light)) +
  ggrepel::geom_text_repel(
    aes(label = id, geometry = geometry),
    stat = "sf_coordinates",
    min.segment.length = 0
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.title = element_blank())





Tomst map.






Code
knitr::include_graphics("figures/plan.png")





Study site plan.




Associated data are:

Macroclimate from ERA5-Land (Muñoz-Sabater et al. 2021) and MétéoFrance
Flux data from Guyaflux (BONAL et al. 2008) representing upper canopy and 2-m conditions and additionally soil water content at different depth
Vegetation from multiple lidar acquisition
Topography extracted from the lidar acquisition
Pedology sampled on the plot

title: Microclimate
text:
Dictionnary
Tomst ancillary informations variables:

site: Nouragues or Paracou
plot: Petit Plateau or P16
subplot: subplot number
tomst_id: tomst ID
tomst_sensornum: tomst manufacturer ID
transect: gap or topography effect
light: gap type
topography: topography type
layon: is it near the layon
date: installation date
operator: Vincyane or Jérôme
closest_trees: closest tree number in Nouragues or the three closest tree numbers and distances for Paracou (number:distance)
gps: gps coordinates in Nouragues
after_gap: before of after the Nouragues’ gap
info_date: date for the information
info: information on the tomst

Granulometry variables:

site: Paracou or Nouragues
topography: topography type
silt, clay, sand: silt, clay, sand content (%)
bulkdensity: bulk density (g/cm3)
a, b, c: coefficient to compute the volumetric moisture content (%) as theta=ax^2+bx+c

Raw Tomst variables (column with no names):

Drop
date: date time every 15 minutes (transform with as.POSIXct and as_datetime)
tz: timezone
t_soil: temperature soil (°C), 8-cm below ground
t_surface: temperature surface (°C), just above ground
t_air: temperature air (°C), 15-cm above ground
moisture: raw electric moisture measurement to convert in swc (soil water content or volumetric moisture (%), 14-cm depth indirect electrical measurement) with swc = amoisture^2 + bmoisture + c

Paracou Tomst variables:

date: date time every 15 minutes
sensornum: tomst manufacturer ID
t_soil: temperature soil (°C), 8-cm below ground
t_surface: temperature surface (°C), just above ground
t_air: temperature air (°C), 15-cm above ground
swc: soil water content or volumetric moisture (%), 14-cm depth indirect electrical measurement

title: Macroclimate
text:
ERA5-Land
We retrieved macroclimatic data from ERA5-Land (Muñoz-Sabater et al. 2021) at both Paracou and at the nearest weather station from Kourou space center (CSG).


Code
era <- read_tsv("data/raw_data/macro/era5.tsv")
g_month <- era %>% 
  group_by(month = month(time)) %>% 
  summarise(tas = mean(tas)) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(month, tas)) +
  geom_rect(xmin = 8, xmax = 12,
            ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf, alpha = 0.5, fill  = "#fff4e0") +
  geom_line() +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.title = element_blank()) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 1:12,
                     labels = c("J", "F", "M", "A", "M", "J", "J", "A",
                                "S", "O", "N", "D"))
g_day <- era %>% 
  group_by(minute = minute(time)+60*hour(time)) %>% 
  summarise(tas = mean(tas)) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(minute/60, tas)) +
  geom_line() +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.title = element_blank())
cowplot::plot_grid(g_month, g_day)





ERA5-Land raw temperature data for Paracou.

title: Macroclimate
text:
Debiaising Hes et al. (2025)
We computed back the correction of Hes et al. (2025) (see section 2 in SI) to determine a and b such \(T_{ec} = \frac{T_e-b}{a}\) in order to correct ERA5-Land with Meteo France weather station data from Kourou CSG at hourly time step.


Code
weather <- list.files("data/raw_data/macro/", pattern = "gz", full.names = TRUE) %>%
  read_delim(locale = locale(decimal_mark = "."), delim = ";") %>% 
  select(NOM_USUEL, LAT, LON, AAAAMMJJHH, RR1, TN50) %>%
  rename_all(tolower) %>%
  rename(loc = nom_usuel, pr = rr1, tas = tn50) %>%
  na.omit() %>% 
  mutate(aaaammjjhh = as.character(aaaammjjhh)) %>%
  mutate(year = str_sub(aaaammjjhh, 1, 4), 
         month = str_sub(aaaammjjhh, 5, 6),
         day = str_sub(aaaammjjhh, 7, 8),
         hour = str_sub(aaaammjjhh, 9, 10)) %>%
  mutate(time = as_datetime(paste0(year, "-", month, "-", day, 
                                   " ", hour, ":00:00"))) %>%
  select(loc, lat, lon, time, tas) %>% 
  filter(loc == "KOUROU CSG") %>% 
  left_join(read_tsv("data/raw_data/macro/era5_station.tsv") %>% 
              select(-lon, -lat),
            suffix = c("_station", "_era"), by = "time") %>% 
  na.omit()
a <- sd(weather$tas_era)/sd(weather$tas_station)
b <- mean(weather$tas_era)-a*mean(weather$tas_station)
weather %>% 
  ggplot(aes(tas_station, tas_era)) +
  geom_abline() +
  geom_point(alpha = .1, aes(col = hour(time))) +
  theme_bw() +
  coord_equal() +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE) +
  ggpubr::stat_regline_equation(
    aes(label =  paste(..eq.label.., ..adj.rr.label.., sep = "~~~~")),
  ) +
  scale_color_viridis_c("Hour of the day") +
  xlab("Weather station hourly temperacture [°C]") +
  ylab("ERA5-Land hourly temperacture [°C]") +
  ggtitle(paste0("a = ", round(a, 2), " | b = ", round(b, 2)))





The temperature relationship between the hourly temperature at the weather station and the hourly temperature at ERA5-Land, as well as the correction factors a and b, following Hes et al. (2025)..





The result differ from Hes et al. (2025) where \(a=0.65\) and \(b=8.72\). ⚠️ this correction as a strong effect on the resulting microclimatic buffer.

However the result is good to remove hour bias but increases bias at monthly and yearly timesteps.


Code
g_month <- weather %>% 
  mutate(tas_era_cor = (tas_era-b)/a) %>% 
  gather(type, tas, -loc, -lon, -lat, -time) %>% 
  group_by(type, month = month(time)) %>% 
  summarise(tas = mean(tas)) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(month, tas, col = type)) +
  geom_rect(xmin = 8, xmax = 12, group = NA, col = NA,
            ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf, alpha = 0.5, fill  = "#fff4e0") +
  geom_line() +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.title = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "bottom") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 1:12,
                     labels = c("J", "F", "M", "A", "M", "J", "J", "A",
                                "S", "O", "N", "D"))
g_day <- weather %>% 
  mutate(tas_era_cor = (tas_era-b)/a) %>% 
  gather(type, tas, -loc, -lon, -lat, -time) %>% 
  group_by(type, minute = minute(time)+60*hour(time)) %>% 
  summarise(tas = mean(tas)) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(minute/60, tas, col = type)) +
  geom_line() +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.title = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "bottom")
cowplot::plot_grid(g_month, g_day)





MeteoFrance and ERA5-Land raw temperature data for Kourou CSG.

title: Macroclimate
text:
Debiaising Fourier - CSG
The problem with the debiasing of Hes et al. (2025) is that it depends on the time-frequency used. We therefore also attempted debiasing using the relationship between the Fourier coefficients after decomposition. We will use the full time series for the Fourier transform, but missing data cannot be allowed for. Our focus will be on the microclimate study years of 2023 and 2024. The weather station data only miss nine measurements on six different days; we will therefore simply fill these in using local linear interpolation. This gives us 365 and 366 days, multiplied by 24 hours (as 2024 is a leap year). This produced a very large power spectrum over two years, with most power at periods of 0, 24 hours, 12 hours and their harmonics, as well as between periods of two years and one day. Looking at the ratio of power between ERA and the weather station, we can see that, as the frequency increases, the ERA power is smaller than that observed. This is logical given that a reanalysis is expected to smooth out small temporal variations. Nonetheless, even at low frequencies and long periods above the day (24-hour period), the power of the weather station tends to be higher than the power of the ERA5-Land reanalysis. We can use this computed ratio for the Kourou CSG station to interpolate the debiased ERA5-Land back at the Paracou station for the years 2023 and 2024.


Code
join <- read_tsv("data/raw_data/macro/era5_station.tsv") %>% 
  arrange(time) %>% 
  filter(time >= as_datetime("2023-04-01 00:00:00")) %>% 
  filter(time <= as_datetime("2025-09-30 23:00:00")) %>% 
  rename(tas_era = tas) %>% 
  select(-lon, -lat) %>% 
  left_join(select(weather, time, tas_station)) %>% 
  mutate(date = as_date(time))
missing <- filter(join, is.na(tas_station)) %>% 
  select(date) %>% 
  unique()
join %>% 
  arrange(time) %>%
  mutate(tas_approx = zoo::na.approx(tas_station)) %>% 
  filter(date %in% missing$date) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = hour(time))) +
  geom_point(aes(y = tas_era, col = "era")) +
  geom_point(aes(y = tas_approx, col = "approx")) +
  geom_point(aes(y = tas_station, col = "station")) +
  facet_wrap(~ date) +
  theme_bw() +
  xlab("") +
  ylab("Temperature [°C]") 





Missing data from the MeteoFrance temperature data from Kourou CSG.






Code
join %>% 
  filter(time < as_datetime("2023-04-07 23:00:00")) %>% 
  ungroup() %>% 
  ggplot(aes(time)) +
  geom_line(aes(y = tas_era, col = "ERA5-Land")) +
  geom_line(aes(y = tas_station, col = "MeteoFrance weather station")) +
  theme_bw() +
  xlab("") + 
  ylab("Temperature [°C]") +
  ggtitle("Centre Spatial Guyanais (CSG)") +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom") +
  scale_color_discrete("")





Caption.






Code
duration <- length(unique(join$date))
fc <- join %>% 
  arrange(time) %>%
  mutate(tas_station = zoo::na.approx(tas_station)) %>% 
  select(-date) %>% 
  gather(source, tas, -time) %>% 
  mutate(source = gsub("tas_", "", source)) %>% 
  group_by(source) %>% 
  do(fft = fft_tab(.$tas, 24*duration)) %>%
  unnest(fft)
g_sub <- fc %>% 
  filter(period != 0) %>% 
  filter(period >= 12) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(frequency, power, fill = source)) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge()) +
  theme_bw() +
  xlab("Period [h]") +
  ylab("Power") +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = c(0, 1/(30*24), 1/(7*24), 1/48, 1/24, 1/12),
    labels = c("0", "1m", "7d", "48", "24", "12")
  ) +
  theme(legend.position = "none", 
        axis.title = element_blank())
fc %>% 
  filter(period != 0) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(frequency, power, fill = source)) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge()) +
  theme_bw() +
  xlab("Period [h]") +
  ylab("Power") +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = c(0, 1 / 24, 1 / 12, 1 / 8, 1 / 6),
    labels = c("0", "24", "12", "8", "6")
  ) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom") +
  annotation_custom(ggplotGrob(g_sub),
                    ymin = 1, ymax = 3.5,
                    xmin = 1/12, xmax = 1/2) +
  annotate("text", x=1/24, y=2, label = paste0("Mean:", round(fc$power[1],2)))





Power spectrums across 1-year for ERA5-Land and MeteoFrance temperature data from Kourou CSG.






Code
ratios <- fc %>% 
  mutate(mode = Mod(fft_coefficient(amplitude, phase))) %>% 
  select(-power, -amplitude, -phase) %>% 
  pivot_wider(names_from = source, values_from = mode) %>% 
  mutate(ratio = station/era)
ratios %>% 
  ggplot(aes(frequency, log(ratio))) +
  geom_point() +
  theme_bw() +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = c(0, 1/(7*24), 1/48, 1/24, 1/12, 1/8, 1/6),
    labels = c("0", "Week", "48", "24", "12", "8", "6")
  ) +
  xlab("Period [h]") +
  ylab("Mod of the ratio of coefficients for Station/Era") +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE) +
    ggpubr::stat_regline_equation(
    aes(label =  paste(..eq.label.., ..adj.rr.label.., sep = "~~~~"))
  )





Power spectrums ratios across 1-year for ERA5-Land and MeteoFrance temperature data from Kourou CSG.

title: Macroclimate
text:
Debiaising Fourier - Paracou
Using adjustment values between Fourier coefficients of the weather station and ERA5-Land we can then de-bias ERA5-Land at the Paracou station. This resulted in higher predicted variation of temperature than the one smoothed by ERA5-Land reanalysis. However note that the extremes might be a bit exaggerated.


Code
era <- read_tsv("data/raw_data/macro/era5.tsv") %>% 
  filter(time >= as_datetime("2023-04-01 00:00:00")) %>% 
  filter(time <= as_datetime("2025-09-30 23:00:00")) %>% 
  select(-lon, -lat)
fc_cor <- fft_tab(era$tas, 24*duration) %>% 
  left_join(select(ratios, frequency, period, ratio)) %>% 
  mutate(coefficient_c = fft_coefficient(amplitude, phase)*ratio)
era$tas_corf <- fft_reconstruct(
  coefficients = fc_cor$coefficient_c,
  frequencies = fc_cor$frequency[-1], 
  times = (1:(24*duration))-1
)
era %>% 
  filter(time < as_datetime("2023-04-07 23:00:00")) %>% 
  gather(source, tas, -time) %>% 
  mutate(source = recode(source,
                         "tas" = "station",
                         "tas_corf" = "Fourier"
                         )) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = time, y = tas, col = source)) +
  geom_line() +
  theme_bw() +
  xlab("") + 
  ylab("Temperature [°C]") 





Interestingly, we find almost back the debaising of Hes et al. (2025) with huorly variation but now the monthly variations are not exaggerated as in Hes et al. (2025), which confirm the interest of the approach for a debiasing at multiple time frequencies.


Code
era <- read_tsv("data/raw_data/macro/era5.tsv") %>% 
  filter(time >= as_datetime("2023-04-01 00:00:00")) %>% 
  filter(time <= as_datetime("2025-09-30 23:00:00")) %>% 
  mutate(tas_cor = (tas-b)/a) %>% 
  rename(raw = tas, hes2025 = tas_cor) 
era$Fourier <- fft_reconstruct(
  coefficients = fc_cor$coefficient_c,
  frequencies = fc_cor$frequency[-1], 
  times = (1:(24*duration))-1
)
era <- era %>% 
  gather(type, tas, -time, -lon, -lat)
write_tsv(era, "data/derived_data/era5_cor.tsv")
g_month <- era %>% 
  group_by(type, month = month(time)) %>% 
  summarise(tas = mean(tas)) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(month, tas, col = type)) +
  geom_rect(xmin = 8, xmax = 12, group = NA, col = NA,
            ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf, alpha = 0.5, fill  = "#fff4e0") +
  geom_line() +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.title = element_blank()) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 1:12,
                     labels = c("J", "F", "M", "A", "M", "J", "J", "A",
                                "S", "O", "N", "D"))
g_day <- era %>% 
  group_by(type, minute = minute(time)+60*hour(time)) %>% 
  summarise(tas = mean(tas)) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(minute/60, tas, col = type)) +
  geom_line() +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.title = element_blank())
cowplot::plot_grid(g_month, g_day, nrow = 2)





ERA5-Land raw and debiased temperature data for Paracou.

title: Vegetation
text:
Plant Area Density (PAD) derived from 1m3 voxels using AMAPVox and interpolated with sensors locations using a 3-m buffer.


Code
library(sf)
library(terra)
library(AMAPVox)
voxels <- readVoxelSpace(
  "data/raw_data/veg/P16_2023_HighAlt_25ha_buffer_PadHLE_intensity1m_testnewEB.vox"
)
voxels@data[, c("x", "y", "z") := getPosition(voxels)[, .(x, y, z)]]
aoi <- read_sf("data/raw_data/inventory/ALT/ALT.shp") %>% 
  st_transform(crs = 2972) %>% 
  st_union()
aoi_m <- as.matrix(aoi[[1]])
matrix <- cbind(aoi_m, c = 0, d = 1)
vop <- as.matrix(read.table("data/raw_data/veg/VOP_P16_25ha.txt"))
local <- matrix %*% t(vop)
local <- local[, -c(3,4)]
X <- range(local[,1]) ; Y <- range(local[,2])
I <- range(voxels@data[x>X[1] & x<X[2] & y>Y[1] & y<Y[2], i]) # 20 119
J <- range(voxels@data[x>X[1] & x<X[2] & y>Y[1] & y<Y[2], j])  # 51 150
K <- range(voxels@data[x>X[1] & x<X[2] & y>Y[1] & y<Y[2], k]) # 0 33
voxels <- AMAPVox::crop(voxels,
                        imin = I[1], imax = I[2],
                        jmin = J[1], jmax = J[2],
                        kmin = K[1], kmax = K[2])
pad <- data.table::setDT(voxels@data)
xyz <- tcrossprod(as.matrix(pad[, .(x, y, z, c=1)]), solve(vop))
pad[, `:=`(Xutm = xyz[, 1], Yutm = xyz[, 2], Zutm = xyz[, 3])]
select(pad, Xutm, Yutm, Zutm, PadBVTotal, ground_distance) %>% 
  mutate(h = round(ground_distance)) %>% 
  filter(h >= 0, h <= 55) %>% 
  rename(x = Xutm, y = Yutm, z = Zutm, pad = PadBVTotal) %>% 
  write_tsv("data/derived_data/pad.tsv")
buffer <- 3
read_tsv("data/derived_data/pad.tsv") %>% 
  select(x, y) %>% 
  unique() %>% 
  rename(x_p = x, y_p = y) %>% 
  mutate(sensors = list(read_tsv("data/derived_data/coords.tsv"))) %>% 
  unnest(sensors) %>% 
  rowwise() %>% 
  mutate(d = sqrt(sum((x-x_p)^2+(y-y_p)^2))) %>% 
  group_by(sensornum) %>% 
  filter(d <= buffer) %>% 
  select(site, plot, subplot, sensornum, id, 
         transect, light, topography, layon,
         x_p, y_p) %>% 
  ungroup() %>% 
  write_tsv("data/derived_data/sensors_pad_xy.tsv")




Code
read_tsv("data/derived_data/pad.tsv") %>% 
  filter(pad < 5) %>% 
  group_by(x, y) %>% 
  summarise(pad = sum(pad, na.rm = TRUE)) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x, y, col = pad)) +
  geom_point() +
  scale_color_viridis_c("Plant Area Density [PAD,"~m^2~m^{-3}~"]", trans = "sqrt") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom", axis.title = element_blank()) +
  coord_equal()

title: Pedology
text:
This is not exploited yet. A lot of soil information is available from different sampling points that can be spatialized similarly to sensors using trilateration with three tree ids and distances. Later this can be krigged with microtopography (e.g. TWI) to be extrapolated at sensors locations.


Code
alt <- read_csv("data/raw_data/inventory/ALT_Paracou9ha_20250129_cleancord.csv") %>% 
  select(IdTree, Xutm, Yutm) %>% 
  rename(id = IdTree, x = Xutm, y = Yutm)
soil <- read_csv("data/raw_data/pedo/Labo_data_binded.csv") %>% 
  mutate(clay = `Argiles %`) %>% 
  mutate(sand = `Sables fins %` + `Sables grossiers %`) %>% 
  mutate(silt = `Limons fins %` + `Limons grossiers %`) %>% 
  select(`Sample ID`, clay, sand, silt,
         TreeNum1, TreeNum2, TreeNum3, DTree1, DTree2, DTree3) %>% 
  rename(soil_id = `Sample ID`,
         id_1 = TreeNum1, id_2 = TreeNum2, id_3 = TreeNum3,
         d_1 = DTree1, d_2 = DTree2, d_3 = DTree3) %>% 
  filter(!is.na(clay)) %>% 
  mutate(d_1 = ifelse(!is.na(id_1) & is.na(d_1), 0, d_1)) %>% 
  mutate(id_d__1 = paste(id_1, d_1),
         id_d__2 = paste(id_2, d_2),
         id_d__3 = paste(id_3, d_3)) %>% 
  select(soil_id, clay, sand, silt, id_d__1, id_d__2, id_d__3) %>% 
  gather(variable, value, -soil_id, -clay, -sand, -silt) %>% 
  separate(value, c("id", "d"), convert = TRUE) %>% 
  separate(variable, c("id_d", "measure"), "__", convert = TRUE) %>% 
  arrange(soil_id, measure) %>% 
  select(-id_d) %>% 
  mutate(id = ifelse(
                  str_length(id) == 6,
                  paste0(str_sub(id, 1,2), "_", str_sub(id, 3,6)),
                  id
                )) %>% 
  na.omit() %>% 
  left_join(alt) %>% 
  na.omit() %>% 
  group_by(soil_id, clay, sand, silt) %>% 
  filter(n() >= 1) %>% 
  do(center = paste(nlm(function(x){
      sqrt(sum((x[1]-.$x)^2+(x[2]-.$y)^2))-sum(.$d)
    }, c(mean(.$x), mean(.$y)))$estimate, collapse = ";")) %>% 
  unnest(center) %>% 
  separate(center, c("x", "y"), sep = ";", convert = TRUE) %>%
  ungroup() %>% 
  gather(variable, value, -soil_id, -x, -y)
ggplot(soil, aes(x, y, col = value)) +
  geom_point() +
  facet_wrap(~ variable) +
  coord_equal() +
  theme_bw() +
  scale_color_viridis_c("[ % ]") +
  theme(axis.title = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "bottom")





Sampled textures.

title: Macroclimate
text:
Comparisons
After debiasing, the ERA5-Land raw temperature showed a small bias compared to the local eddy-flux tower, suggesting that the forest boundary layer may simply have a buffering effect on the observed temperature at the flux tower.


Code
readxl::read_excel("data/raw_data/guyaflux/TourAFlux-2023-2024.xlsx", 
                               sheet = "Gx meteo_2023-2024",
                               col_types = c("date", rep("numeric", 29))) %>% 
  rename_all(tolower) %>% 
  rename(tas = `temp(55)`) %>% 
  select(date, tas) %>% 
  rename(time = date, tas_tower = tas) %>% 
  left_join(read_tsv("data/derived_data/era5_cor.tsv") %>% 
              filter(type == "Fourier")) %>% 
  na.omit() %>% 
  ggplot(aes(tas_tower, tas, col = yday(time))) +
  geom_abline() +
  geom_point() +
  theme_bw() +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE) +
  ggpubr::stat_regline_equation(

Comparison of ERA5-Land temperature against the local eddy-flux tower using hourly measumrents.

title: Macroclimate
text:
Power spectrum
The power spectrum revealed the expected daily strong power at 24-h period but also a lot of power at 12-hr period.


Code
era_f <- read_tsv("data/derived_data/era5_cor.tsv") %>% 
  filter(type == "Fourier") %>% 
  fft_roll(time_window = 5*24,
           time_col = "time",
           temperature_col = "tas")
era_f %>% 
  filter(period != 0) %>% 
  group_by(frequency, period) %>% 
  summarise(sd = sd(power), power = mean(power)) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(frequency, power+1)) +
  geom_col() +
  geom_linerange(aes(ymin = power+1-sd, ymax = power+1+sd)) +
  theme_bw() +
  xlab("Period [h]") +
  ylab("Power") +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = c(0, 1 / 24, 1 / 12, 1 / 8, 1 / 6, 1 / 3),
    labels = c("0", "24", "12", "8", "6", "3")
  ) +
  theme(legend.position.inside = c(0.8, 0.8)) +
  annotate("text", x=3/24, y=6, label = paste0("Mean:", round(era_f$power[1],2)))





Raw ERA5-Land power spectrum.






Code
era_f %>% 
  filter(period == 24) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(time, power)) +
  geom_rect(xmin = as_datetime("2023-8-1 00:00:00"),

            ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf, alpha = 0.25, fill  = "#fff4e0") +
  geom_rect(xmin = as_datetime("2024-8-1 00:00:00"), 
            xmax = as_datetime("2024-12-1 00:00:00"), 
            ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf, alpha = 0.25, fill  = "#fff4e0") +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point(aes(col = month(time) %in% 8:11)) +
  theme_bw() +
  xlab("") +
  ylab("Power for period 24-hr") +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom") +
  scale_color_discrete("Season", labels = c("Wet", "Dry"))





Raw ERA5-Land power for period 24-hr variation across years and seasons.

title: Microclimate
text:
Decomposition
We again find the 24-hr period bearing most powers with increased power in air than surface and low power for soil.


Code
fc_all <- read_tsv("data/derived_data/fc_all.tsv") %>% 
  mutate(type = factor(type, levels = c("air", "surface", "soil")))
t <- fc_all %>% 
  group_by(type, frequency, period) %>% 
  summarise(sd = sd(power), power = mean(power))
t %>% 
  filter(period != 0) %>%
  ggplot(aes(frequency, power)) +
  geom_col() +
  geom_linerange(aes(ymin = power-sd, ymax = power+sd)) +
  geom_text(x = 1/12, y = 2,
            aes(label = paste0("Mean: ", round(power), "±", round(sd))), 
            data = filter(t, period ==0)) +
  theme_bw() +
  xlab("Period [h]") +
  ylab("Power") +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = c(0, 1 / 24, 1 / 12, 1 / 8, 1 / 6, 1 / 3),
    labels = c("0", "24", "12", "8", "6", "3")
  ) +
  theme(legend.position.inside = c(0.8, 0.8)) +
  facet_wrap(~ type, nrow = 3)





Power spectrum across captors.




The power similarly increases during the dry season. Interestingly we can see that the approach is pretty resilient to unfiltered data even if we can easily detect anomalies in soil power due to captors removed from the soil (💡we may want to filter them later).


Code
fc_all %>% 
  filter(period == 24) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(datetime, power)) +
  geom_rect(xmin = as_datetime("2023-8-1 00:00:00"), 
            xmax = as_datetime("2023-12-1 00:00:00"), 
            ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf, alpha = 0.25, fill  = "#fff4e0") +
  geom_rect(xmin = as_datetime("2024-8-1 00:00:00"), 
            xmax = as_datetime("2024-12-1 00:00:00"), 
            ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf, alpha = 0.5, fill  = "#fff4e0") +
  geom_rect(xmin = as_datetime("2025-8-1 00:00:00"), 
            xmax = as_datetime("2025-12-1 00:00:00"), 
            ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf, alpha = 0.5, fill  = "#fff4e0") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = c(as_datetime("2023-8-1 00:00:00"),
                            as_datetime("2024-8-1 00:00:00"),
                            as_datetime("2025-8-1 00:00:00")),
             linetype = "dashed", col = "red") +
  geom_line(aes(group = paste(id, type))) +
  theme_bw() +
  xlab("") +
  ylab("Power for period 24-hr") +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom") +
  facet_wrap(~ type, nrow = 3)





Power for period 24-hr variation across time and seasons.

title: Microclimate
text:
Decomposition 3-months
For comparison, we used a power spectrum to compare our five-day Fourier transform decomposition with a 90-day decomposition using a sliding window of 45 days. Interestingly, we found a very similar spectrum with similar values for the 24 harmonics.


Code

t <- fc_month %>% 
  group_by(type, frequency, period) %>%

t %>%

  ggplot(aes(frequency, power)) +
  geom_col() +
  geom_linerange(aes(ymin = power-sd, ymax = power+sd)) +
  geom_text(x = 1/12, y = 1,
            aes(label = paste0("Mean: ", round(power), "±", round(sd))), 
            data = filter(t, period ==0)) +
  theme_bw() +
  xlab("Period [h]") +
  ylab("Power") +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = c(0, 1 / 24, 1 / 12, 1 / 8, 1 / 6, 1 / 3),

  ) +
  theme(legend.position.inside = c(0.8, 0.8)) +

Power spectrum across captors for 3-months windows sliding by 45 days.

bind_rows(

title: Microclimate
text:
Total power
We quickly compared the total power of each microclimate type with that of the macroclimate, highlighting the dissipation effect of the microclimate, which increases from the air to the soil.


Code
era_f <- read_tsv("data/derived_data/era5_cor.tsv") %>% 
  filter(type == "Fourier") %>% 
  fft_roll(time_window = 5*24,
           time_col = "time",
           temperature_col = "tas") %>% 
  mutate(date = as_date(time)) %>% 
  select(date, period, power) %>% 
  rename(power_era = power)
bind_rows(
  fc_all %>% 
    mutate(date = as_date(datetime)),
  era_f %>% 
    mutate(id = 0, type = "era", power = power_era)
) %>% 
  group_by(id, type, date) %>% 
  summarise(power = sum(power, na.rm = TRUE)) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(type, power)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  theme_bw() +
  xlab("") +
  ylab("Total power")





Total power of macroclimate and microclimate across air, surface and soil captors. Boxplots represent the temporal variation across 5-day windows and spatial variation across plots and forests.

title: Microclimate
text:
Buffer
Comparing HOBO power against ERA5-Land power for the period 24hr we see mainly a buffer effect with decreased amplitude.


Code
read_tsv("data/derived_data/fc_all.tsv") %>% 
  mutate(date = as_date(datetime)) %>% 
  left_join(era_f) %>% 
  filter(!is.na(power_era)) %>% 
  filter(period == 24) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(power_era, power, col = as.factor(id))) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", formula = "y ~ x") +
  theme_bw() +
  xlab("ERA5-Land temperature [°C]") +
  ylab("HOBO temperature [°C]") +
  coord_equal() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom") +
  ggtitle("Period 24-hr") +
  facet_wrap(~ type) +
  scale_color_discrete(guide = "none") +
  geom_abline()





HOBO power against ERA5-Land power for the period 24hr.




The buffer ratio is stronger for soil, then surface and finally air, and stronger in the wet than the dry season.


Code
read_tsv("data/derived_data/fc_all.tsv") %>% 
  mutate(date = as_date(datetime)) %>% 
  left_join(era_f) %>% 
  filter(!is.na(power_era)) %>% 
  filter(period == 24) %>% 
  mutate(ratio = power/power_era) %>% 
  mutate(season = ifelse(month(date) %in% 8:11, "dry", "wet")) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(as.factor(id), ratio, fill = season, col = season)) +
  geom_violin() +
  facet_wrap(~ type, nrow = 3, scales = "free_x") +
  theme_bw() +
  ylab("Amplitude ratio for the period 24-hr") +
  xlab("") +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")





Amplitude ratio for the period 24-hr per sensor season and type.






Code
read_tsv("data/derived_data/fc_all.tsv") %>% 
  mutate(date = as_date(datetime)) %>% 
  left_join(era_f) %>% 
  filter(period %in% c(0,12)) %>% 
  select(-coefficient, -frequency) %>% 
  na.omit() %>% 
  mutate(ratio = power/power_era) %>% 
  select(-power, -power_era) %>% 
  mutate(period = paste0("period_", period)) %>% 
  group_by(id, type, period) %>% 
  summarise(q05 = quantile(ratio, .05),
            q25 = quantile(ratio, .25),
            q5 = quantile(ratio, .5),
            q75 = quantile(ratio, .75),
            q95 = quantile(ratio, .95)) %>% 
  gather(metric, value, -type, -period, -id) %>% 
  mutate(period_metric = paste0(period, "_", metric)) %>% 
  select(-period, -metric) %>%

title: Microclimate
text:
Qualitative descriptors
As expected the buffer ratio is stronger for closed understory than forest gaps with gap borders having intermediate values. This is more marked in wet season and in the air than the soil.


Code
ratios <- read_tsv("data/derived_data/fc_all.tsv") %>% 
  mutate(date = as_date(datetime)) %>% 
  left_join(era_f) %>% 
  filter(!is.na(power_era)) %>% 
  filter(period == 24) %>% 
  mutate(ratio = power/power_era) %>% 
  group_by(type, hydric_year, season, id) %>% 
  summarise(ratio = mean(ratio)) %>% 
  na.omit()

  select(id, light) %>% 
  left_join(ratios) %>% 
  na.omit() %>%

title: Microclimate
text:
Temporal descriptors
We used the Guyaflux tower to explore the temporal characteristics of the microclimate buffer. These characteristics included the daily mean relative humidity at 55 m, the daily total precipitation, the daily mean net solar radiation and the season (factor). Although they are clearly suffering from collinearity, we quickly explored the response of the buffer to each factor independently and together in a linear model. As expected, the buffer showed a strong response to season, with a lower buffer in the wet season. However, it also responded linearly to relative humidity and precipitation: less water in precipitation or in the atmosphere resulted in a better microclimate buffer.

💡 We should test it with VPD and cumulated precipitation on a window and select the model with the best explanatory power.



Code
guyaflux <- readxl::read_excel("data/raw_data/guyaflux/TourAFlux-2023-2024.xlsx", 
                               sheet = "Gx meteo_2023-2024",
                               col_types = c("date", rep("numeric", 29))) %>% 
  rename_all(tolower) %>% 
  rename(hr_55 = `hr(55)`, snet = rg, pr = pluie, swc_0.8 = vwc_avg_80cm) %>% 
  select(date, hr_55, snet, pr, swc_0.8) %>% 
  group_by(date = as_date(date)) %>% 
  mutate(pr = sum(pr)) %>% 
  summarise_all(mean) %>% 
  mutate(pr = log(pr)) %>% 
  gather(variable, value, -date)
temp <- read_tsv("data/derived_data/fc_all.tsv") %>% 
  mutate(date = as_date(datetime)) %>% 
  left_join(era_f) %>% 
  filter(!is.na(power_era)) %>% 
  filter(period == 24) %>% 
  mutate(ratio = power/power_era) %>% 
  filter(type == "air") %>% 
  left_join(guyaflux) %>% 
  filter(!is.na(variable))
ggplot(temp, aes(value, ratio)) +
  geom_point(alpha = .1, aes(col = paste(hydric_year, season))) +
  facet_wrap(~ variable, scales = "free", nrow = 2) +
  theme_bw() +
  ggpubr::stat_regline_equation(
    aes(label =  paste(..eq.label.., ..adj.rr.label.., sep = "~~~~"))
  ) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm") +
  ylab("Mean Amplitude ratio for period 24") +
  xlab("")

era_f <- read_tsv("data/derived_data/era5_cor.tsv") %>% 
  filter(type == "Fourier") %>% 
  fft_roll(time_window = 5*24,
           time_col = "time",
           temperature_col = "tas") %>% 
  mutate(date = as_date(time)) %>% 
  select(date, period, power) %>% 
  rename(power_era = power)
palette <-  c(
  "Air"     = "#56B4E9",   # sky blue
  "Surface" = "#009E73",   # orange
  "Soil"    = "#E69F00"    # green
)
g <- read_tsv("data/derived_data/fc_all.tsv") %>% 
  mutate(date = as_date(datetime)) %>% 
  left_join(era_f) %>% 
  filter(period %in% c(0,12)) %>% 
  select(-coefficient, -frequency) %>% 
  na.omit() %>% 
  mutate(ratio = power/power_era) %>% 
  select(-power, -power_era) %>% 
  mutate(period = paste0("period_", period)) %>% 
  na.omit() %>% 
  group_by(id, type, period, hydric_year, season) %>% 
  summarise(q05 = quantile(ratio, .05),
            q25 = quantile(ratio, .25),
            q5 = quantile(ratio, .5),
            q75 = quantile(ratio, .75),
            q95 = quantile(ratio, .95)) %>% 
  gather(metric, value, -type, -period, -id, -hydric_year, -season) %>% 
  mutate(period_metric = paste0(period, "_", metric)) %>% 
  ungroup() %>% 
  select(-period, -metric) %>% 
  pivot_wider(names_from = period_metric, values_from = value) %>% 
  mutate(season_long = recode(season, "dry" = "Dry season", "wet" = "Wet season")) %>% 
  mutate(hydric_year_long = recode(hydric_year, 
                                   "2023-2024" = "El Niño year\n2023-2024",
                                   "2024-2025" = "Normal year\n2024-2025")) %>% 
  mutate(type = factor(type, levels = c("air", "surface", "soil"))) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(period_0_q5, period_12_q5, col = type)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 1, col = "grey") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 1, col = "grey") +
  geom_segment(aes(x = period_0_q25, xend = period_0_q75), alpha = .5) +
  geom_segment(aes(x = period_0_q05, xend = period_0_q95), linewidth = .2, alpha = .5) +
  geom_segment(aes(y = period_12_q25, yend = period_12_q75), alpha = .5) +
  geom_segment(aes(y = period_12_q05, yend = period_12_q95), linewidth = .2, alpha = .5) +
  geom_point() +
  theme_bw() +
  xlab("Mean temperature ratio") +
  ylab("24-h amplitude ratio") +
  facet_grid(hydric_year_long ~ season_long) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom") +
  scale_color_discrete("", palette = palette) +
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 0, size = 12),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12),
        strip.text = element_text(size = 12),
        axis.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
        legend.text = element_text(size = 12)) +
  annotate("segment", x = 1.001, y = 0.9, xend = 1.05, yend = 0.9,
           arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.5, "cm"), type = "closed"),
           size = 1.1, col = "darkgrey") +
  annotate("text", x = 1.025, y = 0.85,
           col = "darkgrey", size = 5, label = "amplifying") +
  annotate("segment", x = 0.999, y = 0.9, xend = 0.95, yend = 0.9,
           arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.5, "cm"), type = "closed"),
           size = 1.1, col = "darkgrey") +
  annotate("text", x = 0.98, y = 0.85,
           col = "darkgrey", size = 5, label = "buffering")

palette <-  c(
  "Air"     = "#56B4E9",   # sky blue
  "Surface" = "#009E73",   # orange
  "Soil"    = "#E69F00"    # green
)
ratios <- read_tsv("data/derived_data/fc_all.tsv") %>% 
  mutate(date = as_date(datetime)) %>% 
  left_join(era_f) %>% 
  filter(!is.na(power_era)) %>% 
  filter(period %in% c(0, 24)) %>% 
  mutate(ratio = power/power_era) %>% 
  group_by(period, type, hydric_year, season, id) %>% 
  summarise(ratio = mean(ratio)) %>% 
  na.omit()
pad <- read_tsv("data/derived_data/sensors_pad_xy.tsv") %>% 
  rename(x = x_p, y = y_p) %>%
  left_join(read_tsv("data/derived_data/pad.tsv")) %>% 
  group_by(id, h) %>% 
  summarise(pad = median(pad)) %>% 
  mutate(strata = ifelse(h < 10, "understory", "upperstory")) %>% 
  group_by(id, strata) %>% 
  summarise(pad = sum(pad)) %>% 
  mutate(strata = paste0("pad_", strata)) %>% 
  pivot_wider(names_from = "strata", values_from = "pad")
topo <- read_tsv("data/derived_data/coords.tsv") %>% 
  mutate(twi = extract(rast("data/raw_data/topo/Paracou_ALT_TWI_5m.tif"),
                       read_tsv("data/derived_data/coords.tsv")[2:3])$Paracou_TWI_5m) %>% 
  select(id, twi)
model_all <- ratios %>% 
  left_join(pad) %>% 
  left_join(topo) %>% 
  mutate(season = factor(season, levels = c("wet", "dry"))) %>% 
  mutate(hydric_year = recode(hydric_year, 
                               "2024-2025" = "normal", 
                              "2023-2024" = "El Nino")) %>% 
  mutate(hydric_year = factor(hydric_year, levels = c("normal", "El Nino"))) %>% 
  ungroup() %>% 
  mutate(across(c("twi", "pad_understory", "pad_upperstory"), scale)) %>% 
  nest_by(period, type) %>% 
  mutate(
    fit = list(lm(ratio ~ season*hydric_year + twi + pad_understory + pad_upperstory, data = data)),
    glance = list(broom::glance(fit) %>% 
                    select(adj.r.squared, nobs)),
    estimates = list(broom::tidy(fit, conf.int = TRUE) %>% 
                       select(term, estimate, p.value, conf.low, conf.high))
  ) %>% 
  select(type, glance, estimates) %>% 
  unnest() %>% 
  filter(term != "(Intercept)") %>% 
  rename_all(~ gsub(".", "_", ., fixed = TRUE)) %>% 
  mutate(period = recode(as.character(period),
                         "0" = "Mean temperature ratio",
                         "24" = "24-h amplitude ratio")) %>% 
  mutate(period = factor(period, levels = c("Mean temperature ratio",
                                            "24-h amplitude ratio"))) %>% 
  mutate(type = factor(type, levels = c("air", "surface", "soil"))) %>% 
  mutate(term = recode(term,
                       "twi" = "TWI",
                       "seasondry" = "Dry season",
                       "hydric_yearEl Nino" = "El Niño year",
                       "pad_understory" = "PAD understory",
                       "pad_upperstory" = "PAD upperstory",
                       "seasondry:hydric_yearEl Nino" = "Dry season x El Niño year"
                       )) %>% 
  mutate(term = factor(term, levels = c("Dry season",
                                        "El Niño year",
                                        "Dry season x El Niño year",
                                        "TWI",
                                        "PAD understory",
                                        "PAD upperstory") %>% 
                         rev()))
rsquared <- select(model_all, adj_r_squared) %>% unique()
g <- model_all %>% 
  ggplot(aes(term, estimate, col = type, alpha = (p_value < 0.05))) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, col = "darkgrey") +
  geom_segment(aes(y = conf_low, yend = conf_high), 
               position = position_dodge(width = 0.3, reverse = TRUE)) +
  geom_point(position = position_dodge(width = 0.3, reverse = TRUE)) +
  theme_bw() +
  xlab("") + ylab("") +
  scale_alpha_discrete(guide = "none", range = c(0.2, 1)) +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_color_discrete("", palette = palette) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom") +
  facet_wrap(~ period) +
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 0, size = 12),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12),
        strip.text = element_text(size = 12),
        axis.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
        legend.text = element_text(size = 12)) +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0("R² = ",  round(adj_r_squared, 2))), 
            position = position_dodge(width = 1, reverse = TRUE), 
            hjust = 0, show.legend = FALSE,
            data = rsquared %>%  mutate(p_value = 0.01,
                                        term = "Dry season", estimate = 0.05)) +
  annotate("segment", x = 0.6, y = 0.01, xend = 0.6, yend = 0.1,
           arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.5, "cm"), type = "closed"),
           size = 0.9, col = "darkgrey") +
  annotate("segment", x = 0.6, y = -0.01, xend = 0.6, yend = -0.06,
           arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.5, "cm"), type = "closed"),
           size = 0.9, col = "darkgrey") +
  annotate("text", x = 0.75, y = -0.02,
           size = 3, col = "darkgrey", label = "+buffer") +
  annotate("text", x = 0.75, y = 0.06,
           size = 3, col = "darkgrey", label = "-buffer")
cowplot::save_plot("figures/f4.png",
                 plot = g,
                 base_width = 7,
                 base_height = 5,
                 dpi = 300,
                 bg = "white")
